title: Phase 1: Project Setup & Core Models
Create all models with validations and associations
description: Implement Player, Game, Rating, Role, Award, PlayerAward models with validations, associations, scopes, computed attributes

title: Player profile - news mentions
description: Show news mentioning a player on their profile page. GH #196.

title: Telegram bot integration for news
description: Bot that posts messages from Telegram chat as news articles. Parent issue. GH #197.
close_reason: All child tasks completed: message parser, webhook endpoint, author whitelist, admin UI, background job

title: Interactive game protocol mode - live auto-save
description: Interactive/live mode for protocol form with auto-save via Stimulus+Turbo. GH #198.

title: ActionCable channel for game protocol updates
description: GameProtocolChannel that broadcasts updates when protocol is saved. GH #199.

title: OBS overlay page - game state view
description: Standalone page showing current game state for OBS browser source. GH #200.

title: OBS overlay styling and configuration
description: Style overlay for OBS: transparent bg, readable fonts, URL params for customization. GH #201.

title: OBS integration documentation and judge guide
description: Document OBS browser source setup and judge guide for live streams. GH #202.

title: Register Telegram bot and store API token
description: Register bot via BotFather, store token in Rails credentials. GH #203.

title: Telegram webhook endpoint controller
description: Webhooks::TelegramController to receive POST from Telegram. GH #204.

title: Register webhook URL with Telegram API
description: Rake task to call Telegram setWebhook API. GH #205.

title: Write model specs
description: Write RSpec tests for all model validations, associations, scopes, and computed attributes using Shoulda Matchers

title: Telegram message parser service
description: Service to extract data from Telegram webhook payload. GH #206.

title: Telegram author whitelist
description: TelegramAuthor model to whitelist Telegram users who can create news. GH #207.

title: Process Telegram message background job
description: Job to parse message, check whitelist, check news tag, create draft News. GH #208.
close_reason: PR #328 implements the Telegram message processing background job

title: Download and attach Telegram media to News articles
description: Download Telegram photos/videos via Bot API and attach to News via ActiveStorage. GH #209.

title: Notify editors when Telegram news draft is created
description: Notify editors when draft News is created from Telegram message. GH #210.

title: Admin UI for Telegram bot settings
description: Admin page for Telegram bot config: webhook status, news tag, author whitelist. GH #211.
close_reason: PR #325 adds admin UI for Telegram bot settings

title: Set up Sentry error reporting
description: Add sentry-ruby and sentry-rails gems, configure initializer with DSN from env, breadcrumbs, traces sampling. Enable only in production. Register as Rails 8 error reporter. GH #226.

title: Add optional series association to News model
description: Add optional belongs_to :series to News. Add series_id FK column, index, and for_series scope. GH #227.

title: Add series selector to news admin form
description: Add series dropdown to admin news form, update strong params, i18n, and specs. GH #228.

title: Series page - linked news section
description: Show published news linked to a series on the series page. Use for_series scope. Similar to vanilla-mafia-99 (game detail page). GH #230.

title: Seed roles dictionary and sample data
description: Create db/seeds.rb with role codes (peace, mafia, don, sheriff) and sample admin user

title: Competition model, migration, factory, and specs
description: Create Competition model with self-referential parent_id tree. Columns: parent_id (FK, nullable), kind (string enum: season/series/minicup/tournament/round/group/fun_session), name (string, required), slug (string, unique), position (integer), started_on/ended_on (date), featured (boolean), legacy_season/legacy_series (integer, temporary). Add factory with traits for each kind, model specs for validations/associations/scopes. GH #231.

title: Populate competitions from existing season/series game data
description: Data migration: for each distinct season in games, create a season Competition. For each (season, series) pair, create a child series Competition. Set legacy_season/legacy_series for traceability. Set featured=true on seasons. GH #232.

title: Add competition_id FK to games table and backfill
description: Add nullable competition_id column to games. Backfill from legacy_season/legacy_series matching on competitions. Add index. Do NOT make NOT NULL yet. GH #233.

title: Add competition_id FK to player_awards and backfill
description: Add nullable competition_id column to player_awards. Backfill from season column matching season-kind competitions. Add index. GH #234.

title: Add optional competition_id FK to news table
description: Add nullable competition_id column to news table with index. News can optionally link to a competition (e.g., series recap). No backfill needed. GH #235.

title: Update Game model: competition association, validations, scopes
description: Add belongs_to :competition to Game. Update uniqueness validation from (season, series, game_number) to (competition_id, game_number). Replace for_season scope. Update ordered scope. Replace available_seasons with Competition.featured query. GH #236.

title: Update Game#full_name and #in_season_name for competitions
description: Rewrite full_name and in_season_name to derive from competition name instead of hardcoded Russian strings. Use i18n for formatting. GH #237.

title: Update PlayerAward model: competition association
description: Add belongs_to :competition, optional: true to PlayerAward. Update uniqueness validation scope from season to competition_id. GH #238.
close_reason: PR #331 adds competition association to PlayerAward

title: Update News model: add competition association
description: Add belongs_to :competition, optional: true to News. Add for_competition scope. GH #239.
close_reason: PR #324 merges competition association and for_competition scope into News model

title: Player model: with_stats_for_competition scope
description: Replace with_stats_for_season(season) scope with with_stats_for_competition(competition) that queries using competition.subtree_ids to aggregate stats across a competition tree. GH #240.

title: Write acceptance tests for public pages
description: Write Capybara acceptance tests for season overview, game details, player profile, series totals, hall of fame

title: CompetitionOverviewService (replaces Season + Series services)
description: Create CompetitionOverviewService that replaces both SeasonOverviewService and SeriesAggregationService. For parent competitions: group games by child competition. For leaf competitions: show games directly with player totals table. Use competition subtree for stats aggregation. GH #241.

title: Update PlayerProfileService: group games by root competition
description: Change game grouping from game.season integer to root competition. Eager-load competition associations to avoid N+1. GH #242.

title: Update HallOfFameService: competition-aware awards
description: Update award display to use competition.name instead of season integer. GH #243.

title: CompetitionsController (replaces Seasons + Series controllers)
description: Create CompetitionsController#show that finds competition by slug. Delegates to CompetitionOverviewService. Replaces SeasonsController and SeriesController. GH #244.

title: Routes: competition routes and legacy season/series redirects
description: Add /competitions/:slug route. Add redirect routes from /seasons/:number and /seasons/:number/series/:number to corresponding competition slugs. Update root route. GH #245.

title: Competition show view (replaces seasons and series views)
description: Create competitions/show view that renders parent competitions (with children table) and leaf competitions (with games/player totals). Replace seasons/show and series/show. GH #246.

title: Update layout navigation to use Competition.featured
description: Replace available_seasons helper with Competition.featured.ordered in both mobile and desktop navigation. Link to competition_path(slug:). GH #247.

title: Update player profile view: group by competition
description: Update players/show to group games by root competition instead of season integer. Update column headers and labels. GH #248.

title: Update hall of fame view: show competition name
description: Update hall_of_fame/_award partial to display competition.name instead of season integer. GH #249.

title: Update game detail view: competition-based breadcrumb
description: Update games/show to show competition-based breadcrumb/title instead of hardcoded season/series display. GH #250.

title: Implement SeasonsController#show
description: Season overview page with games grouped by series and player rankings sorted by total rating

title: Update protocol form: competition dropdown replaces season/series
description: Replace season and series number inputs in judge/protocols/_form with a competition dropdown select. Update controller strong params and service. GH #251.

title: Avo admin: Competition resource
description: Create Avo resource for Competition with fields for parent, kind, name, slug, position, dates, featured. Enable CRUD. GH #252.

title: Update Avo Game and PlayerAward resources for competitions
description: Replace season/series fields with competition belongs_to in Avo Game resource. Replace season field with competition in PlayerAward resource. GH #253.

title: Remove legacy columns: season/series from games, season from player_awards
description: Final cleanup migration: remove season, series columns from games table. Remove season from player_awards. Remove legacy_season, legacy_series from competitions. Drop old indexes. GH #254.

title: Add evilution gem alongside mutant
description: Add evilution gem to Gemfile, verify it works, document CLI usage. GH #259.

title: Update agent instructions: run evilution before mutant
description: Update agent instructions to run evilution first, then mutant. Data collection phase. GH #260.

title: Evaluate: reverse order — mutant first, evilution second
description: Reverse run order to mutant→evilution, compare efficiency. Manual evaluation checkpoint. GH #261.

title: Remove mutant gem, switch fully to evilution
description: Remove mutant-rspec, update all instructions to evilution only. GH #262.

title: Add evilution mutation testing step to CI pipeline
description: Add evilution to CI workflow. Run on changed files only. GH #263.

title: Fix Avo PlayerClaim show page crash: missing keyword :resource
description: Avo 3.30.0 executes visible lambdas via instance_exec in ExecutionContext, so keyword args like resource: are not passed. Use bare lambda with record method instead.

title: Implement GamesController#show
description: Game details page with rating table showing all players, roles, scores

title: Protocol: make result field selectable from two options
description: Replace free-text result input in judge protocol form with a selector for the two game outcomes: Победа мирных / Победа мафии. GH #290.

title: Refactor Game result to enum with in_progress default
description: Replace nullable string result with enum :result backed by Hash constant { in_progress: 'in_progress', peace_victory: 'Победа мирных', mafia_victory: 'Победа мафии' }. Add data migration to backfill NULL results to in_progress, make column NOT NULL with default. Update GameParticipation#result, GameScraper, and protocol form to use constants. GH #TBD.

title: Avo Competition resource: add slug and position fields
description: The Avo Competition resource was added as a minimal resource for the Game belongs_to dropdown. It needs slug and position fields so admins can manage competitions properly without hitting slug uniqueness errors or unexpected dropdown ordering.

title: Update agent instructions: run evilution before mutant
description: Update .claude-on-rails/context.md and prompts/tests.md to run evilution first, then mutant. After each PR, note how many additional fixes mutant catches that evilution missed. Data collection phase for GH #183 (Switch to evilution).

title: Admin telegram: add user select to author whitelist form
description: The add-author form at /admin/telegram only has telegram_user_id and telegram_username fields. Add a user dropdown so admins can link a TelegramAuthor to a User directly from the UI, instead of requiring a Rails console session.
close_reason: PR #330 adds user select to telegram author whitelist form

title: Remove legacy season/series columns from news table
description: The news table still has season and series columns (plus index). These should be removed now that competitions are the canonical reference. Update News model (remove validations, for_series scope), SeriesController references in views, admin news form/show, and all related specs.

title: Online mafia game - epic
description: Major feature: online mafia game with 10 players + optional judge connected via video/audio. Uses LiveKit (start with cloud free tier, migrate to self-hosted later). Key layers: video/audio (LiveKit SFU), signaling (ActionCable), game logic (roles, phases, voting, timers, elimination), frontend UI (video grid, game controls, judge dashboard). This is a brainstorming epic — sub-issues to be defined.

title: Improve all pages — review and fix every view in the system
description:
Epic: review every page in the system (except AVO admin) and collect a list of fixes and improvements for each.

## Pages to review

### Public pages
1. **Competition page** — `/competitions/season-5`
2. **Game page** — `/games/1`
3. **Player profile** — `/players/1`
4. **News index** — `/news`
5. **News show** — `/news/1`
6. **Hall of Fame** — `/hall`
7. **OBS overlay** — `/games/1/overlay`
8. **Error pages** — `/404`, `/422`, `/500`

### Authenticated pages (any user)
9. **Edit profile** — `/profile/edit`
10. **Notification settings** — `/notification_settings/edit`
11. **Player dispute form** — `/players/1/dispute/new`

### Judge pages
12. **New protocol** — `/judge/protocols/new`
13. **Edit protocol (with autosave)** — `/judge/protocols/1/edit`

### Editor/Admin pages
14. **Admin news index** — `/admin/news`
15. **Admin news new** — `/admin/news/new`
16. **Admin news show** — `/admin/news/1`
17. **Admin news edit** — `/admin/news/1/edit`
18. **Telegram settings** — `/admin/telegram`

### Auth pages (Devise)
19. **Sign in** — `/users/sign_in`
20. **Sign up** — `/users/sign_up`
21. **Forgot password** — `/users/password/new`
22. **Reset password** — `/users/password/edit`
23. **Edit registration** — `/users/edit`

For each page, review and note: layout issues, missing i18n, accessibility, mobile responsiveness, UX improvements, visual polish, and any bugs.

description: Player profile with per-season breakdown, game history, awards section

title: Pagination where needed

description: Eager loading, caching, database index verification

title: Docker configuration
description: Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml for development and production

title: GitHub Actions CI pipeline
description: CI workflow: lint (rubocop), test (rspec), security (brakeman, bundler-audit)
close_reason: CI pipeline already configured in .github/workflows/ci.yml — lint, test, and security scanning all in place.

title: Production configuration
description: Rails encrypted credentials, logging, caching config
close_reason: PR #39 - configured SSL, Hetzner VPS IP, force_ssl

title: Data migration from old PostgreSQL database
description: Rake task to import data from old chimafia PostgreSQL database into new SQLite schema
close_reason: Implemented web scraper for data import. PR #32

title: Fix CI: exclude pending acceptance tests from test job
Re-include acceptance tests in CI after views are implemented
description: Remove the spec/acceptance/ exclusion from .github/workflows/ci.yml once all 5 public page views are done (vanilla-mafia-14 through 18). All 22 acceptance tests must pass in CI.

title: Refactor specs to use let declarations and contexts
description: Tech debt: refactor existing RSpec tests to use let/let! declarations and nested context blocks instead of inline variable declarations inside test bodies. This improves readability, reduces duplication, and follows RSpec best practices.

title: Phase 4: Polish & Deployment
description: Error handling, pagination, performance optimization, Docker, CI/CD, production config, data migration from old PostgreSQL database.
close_reason: All Phase 4 sub-tasks completed: pagination, performance optimization, indexes, caching, dynamic nav, deployment config

title: Add test-prof gem and use let_it_be in specs
description: Install the test-prof gem and prefer let_it_be over let!/let for test data that does not need to be recreated per example. This reduces test suite runtime by creating records once per describe/context group. Update development instructions in .claude-on-rails/context.md accordingly.

title: Choose deployment platform
description: Evaluate and choose a hosting platform for production deployment (e.g., Hetzner, DigitalOcean, Railway, Fly.io, Render). Consider: cost, SQLite compatibility, ease of deployment, SSL, custom domains.
close_reason: Hetzner VPS chosen as deployment platform (with both IPv4 and IPv6)

title: Configure deployment pipeline
description:
Set up automated deployment from GitHub Actions (CD). Includes:
- GitHub Actions workflow triggered on push to master (or tag creation)
- Build Docker image and push to ghcr.io
- Run kamal deploy from the CI runner
- Fix .kamal/secrets to support both local (gh CLI) and CI (GITHUB_TOKEN) auth
- Database migrations, health checks, zero-downtime deploys
- DEPLOY_SERVER passed as a GitHub Actions secret

title: Claim Player Profile feature
description: Allow registered users to claim a player profile (1:1 link). Includes: pending/approved flow, profile editing (comment + photo), Avo admin for reviewing claims. Full plan in transcript dd1d4d90.

title: Migrations + config initializer for player claims
description: Create two migrations: (1) add_player_id_to_users — add_reference :users, :player with unique index, null: true, foreign_key: true. (2) create_player_claims — table with user/player refs, status (pending/approved/rejected), rejection_reason, reviewed_by, reviewed_at, plus unique index on [user_id, player_id] and index on status. Also create config/initializers/player_claims.rb with require_approval = true setting.

title: PlayerClaim model + factory + specs + mutant
description: Create PlayerClaim model with: belongs_to :user, belongs_to :player, belongs_to :reviewed_by (User, optional). Validates status inclusion in %w[pending approved rejected], uniqueness of [user_id, player_id]. Custom validations: user has no existing claimed player, player not already claimed. Scopes: .pending, .approved. Status predicates: pending?, approved?, rejected?. Class method: .require_approval? reading from Rails config. Add factory in spec/factories/player_claims.rb (default status pending). Write model specs and run mutant for zero surviving mutants.

title: User + Player model updates for claims
description: User model: add belongs_to :player (optional), has_many :player_claims (dependent: destroy), validates :player_id uniqueness (allow_nil). Helpers: claimed_player?, pending_claim?, pending_claim_for(player). Player model: add has_one :user, has_many :player_claims (dependent: destroy). Helpers: claimed?, claimed_by?(user). Update existing specs and run mutant.

title: ClaimPlayerService + ReviewClaimService
description: ClaimPlayerService: follows Data.define + .call pattern. Returns Result(success, claim, error). Logic: if user already has player → error :already_has_player; if player already claimed → error :player_already_claimed; if require_approval? AND user is NOT admin → create pending PlayerClaim; if user IS admin OR approval not required → transaction: create approved PlayerClaim + set user.player_id. ReviewClaimService: two class methods .approve(claim:, admin:) and .reject(claim:, admin:, reason:). Approve: transaction, update claim status + set user.player_id. Reject: update claim status + store rejection reason. Write service specs and run mutant.
close_reason: Already implemented and merged to master

title: Pundit policies for claims + profile
description: PlayerClaimPolicy: create? — user present, not already claimed, no pending claim. index?/show?/update?/destroy? — admin only (for Avo). ProfilePolicy: edit?/update? — user owns the player (user.player_id == player.id). Write policy specs.
close_reason: Already implemented and merged to master

title: Flash messages + auth links in layout
description: Layout changes in app/views/layouts/application.html.erb: (1) Flash messages — add before yield in main: green for notice, red for alert, Tailwind-styled, auto-dismiss. (2) Auth links in both sidebar nav and mobile nav: signed in → Выйти (sign out, turbo_method: :delete); not signed in → Войти (sign in link). (3) Admin link: signed-in admin → Админка link to /avo, visible in both sidebar and mobile nav. Use i18n keys nav.sign_in / nav.sign_out / nav.admin.
close_reason: Already implemented and merged to master

title: Configure RSpec, FactoryBot, Capybara, SimpleCov
description: Install testing gems, run rspec:install, configure rails_helper.rb with FactoryBot, Shoulda Matchers, SimpleCov, DatabaseCleaner

title: PlayerClaimsController + routes + request specs
description: Routes: resources :players, only: [:show] do; resource :claim, only: [:create], controller: 'player_claims'; end. Controller: before_action :authenticate_user\!, create action finds Player, calls ClaimPlayerService, redirects back with flash. Write request specs covering: auth guard, claim with/without approval, duplicate claims.

title: ProfilesController + edit view + request specs
description: Routes: resource :profile, only: [:edit, :update]. Controller: before_action :authenticate_user!, before_action :ensure_claimed_player (redirects if current_user.player_id nil). edit: loads current_user.player. update: updates comment and photo via strong params, redirects to player show. View: simple form with comment textarea, photo file field with current preview, submit button, error display. Write request specs covering: auth guard, no claimed player redirect, edit/update happy path.

title: Player show view: claim button + edit link
description: Add conditional block after h1 on player show page: (1) User owns this player → Редактировать профиль link to edit_profile_path. (2) Unclaimed player, user eligible → Это мой профиль button_to with Turbo confirm. (3) User has pending claim for this player → Ваша заявка ожидает подтверждения text. (4) Otherwise → nothing. Use i18n keys under players.show.

title: Avo admin for PlayerClaims
description: Avo::Resources::PlayerClaim: fields for user (belongs_to), player (belongs_to), status (badge: pending=warning, approved=success, rejected=danger), rejection_reason, reviewed_by, reviewed_at. Avo actions: ApproveClaim — visible when pending, calls ReviewClaimService.approve. RejectClaim — visible when pending, has optional rejection_reason field, calls ReviewClaimService.reject. Update existing Avo resources: User — add field :player as :belongs_to. Player — add field :user as :has_one.
close_reason: Already implemented and merged to master

title: i18n keys for player claims feature
description: Add Russian locale keys to config/locales/ru.yml: nav.sign_in / nav.sign_out / nav.admin, player_claims.create.pending / .approved / .errors.*, players.show.claim_player / .claim_confirm / .claim_pending / .edit_profile, profiles.edit.title / .save / .name_label / .name_hint, profiles.update.success, profiles.errors.no_claimed_player, devise.registrations/sessions/passwords keys as needed for custom views.
close_reason: Already implemented and merged to master

title: Dispute an already-claimed player profile
description: Allow a user to dispute a player profile that was already claimed by another user. This would let the real person reclaim their profile if someone else claimed it first. Design TBD — possibly creates a special dispute claim that notifies admins for manual resolution.

title: Fix SQL injection in import rake task
description: PR #32 review: table name interpolated directly into SQL in import.rake:161. Use ActiveRecord's reset_pk_sequence\! or sanitize the table name. Security issue.

title: Validate content type and base64 in scrapers
description: PR #32 review: (1) player_scraper.rb:27 and import.rake:210 — content_type from scraped data used without validation, should whitelist image types. (2) player_scraper.rb:29 and import.rake:211 — use Base64.strict_decode64 instead of decode64 to catch invalid input.

title: Add HTTP timeouts and error handling to scraper
description: PR #32 review: (1) base.rb:13 — no explicit timeouts on Net::HTTP requests, could hang indefinitely across ~320 requests. Add open_timeout and read_timeout. (2) base.rb:21 — no rescue for network errors (SocketError, Timeout::Error, Errno::ECONNREFUSED). Wrap in rescue block.

title: Improve scraper parsing robustness
description: PR #32 review: (1) hall_scraper.rb:110 — fragile DOM parsing for staff section, use more robust selectors like heading text lookup. (2) game_scraper.rb:59 — unmapped roles silently produce nil role_code, should log warning and skip. (3) season_scraper.rb:39 — played_on stored as raw string without date parsing/validation.

title: Configure Devise for admin authentication
description: Install Devise, generate User model with admin flag, configure mailer defaults

title: Make scraper season range configurable
description: PR #32 review: season_scraper.rb:6 — seasons 1-5 hardcoded in loop. Make configurable via parameter or environment variable, or implement dynamic season discovery.

title: Scraper minor cleanup
description: PR #32 review minor items: (1) import.rake:5 — add explicit require for stringio and base64. (2) base.rb:33 — use BigDecimal instead of to_f for decimal parsing to match DB column type. (3) game_scraper.rb:36 — remove unnecessary /i flag on Cyrillic regex. (4) import.rake:81 — move Active Storage file attachments outside DB transaction.

title: Add test coverage for scraper classes
description: PR #32 review: lib/scraper/ classes have no tests. Add specs with sample HTML fixtures to verify parsing logic, error handling, and data transformations for BaseScraper, SeasonScraper, GameScraper, PlayerScraper, and HallScraper.

title: DB-backed application settings model
description: Create a Setting model for runtime-configurable application settings, editable via Avo admin. Key/value store (e.g. key: string, value: string) with type casting helpers. Replace config/initializers/player_claims.rb with a DB-backed setting so admins can toggle require_approval without restarting the app. Include Avo resource for managing settings.

title: Migrate player_claims.require_approval to DB setting
description: Once vanilla-mafia-63 (Setting model) is done, migrate the require_approval toggle from config/initializers/player_claims.rb to a DB-backed setting. Update PlayerClaim.require_approval? to read from Setting instead of Rails.application.config. Remove the initializer.

title: Configure backup from Hetzner to local or OneDrive cloud
description: Set up automated backups of the Hetzner-hosted application (database and assets) to either local storage or OneDrive cloud. Evaluate both options and implement the most suitable one.

title: Configure email/SMTP provider for production
description: Choose and configure an email provider (e.g., Mailgun, Postmark, SendGrid) for transactional emails in production. Set up Rails Action Mailer with SMTP credentials in encrypted credentials.
close_reason: Configured Resend SMTP relay for transactional emails

title: Choose Docker registry for production images
description: Evaluate Docker registry options (Docker Hub, GitHub Container Registry, Hetzner Container Registry) and configure Kamal deploy.yml accordingly. Consider: cost, ease of setup, integration with CI/CD.
close_reason: PR #40 - ghcr.io configured as Docker registry

title: Choose and configure domain name
description: Pick a domain name for the production app. Update config/deploy.yml (proxy.host) and config/environments/production.rb (action_mailer.default_url_options) — both currently have app.example.com as placeholder.
notes: Postponing domain name — using Hetzner IP (46.62.146.40) directly for initial deploy. Will revisit domain + SSL later.
close_reason: Deferred — using IP address for initial deploy. Will revisit domain + SSL later.

title: Set up GitHub PAT for ghcr.io on Hetzner VPS
description: Before first Kamal deploy, the Hetzner VPS needs a way to authenticate with ghcr.io. Either install gh CLI on the server or switch .kamal/secrets to use an environment variable with a GitHub PAT (write:packages scope). Currently .kamal/secrets uses gh config get which only works locally.
close_reason: Folded into vanilla-mafia-42 — the real issue is CI runner auth, not VPS auth. Kamal handles docker login on the server automatically.

title: Configure Pundit for authorization
description: Install Pundit, generate base ApplicationPolicy, create admin policy

title: Wire ADMIN_EMAIL and ADMIN_PASSWORD through Kamal deploy
description: Add admin credentials to deploy workflow env, .kamal/secrets, and config/deploy.yml so the production container receives them for db:seed.

title: Investigate SQLite database persistence across deploys
description: SQLite database may be created inside the Docker image instead of on the mounted volume, which would cause data loss on every deploy. Verify that the database files are stored in the vanilla_mafia_storage volume (mounted at /rails/storage) and not in the default Rails db/ directory inside the container.
close_reason: Verified: storage/ is mounted as a Docker named volume (vanilla_mafia_storage:/rails/storage). All SQLite databases are on the persistent volume and survive deploys.

title: Get domain name and configure DNS + SSL
description: Choose and register a domain name, point DNS to the Hetzner VPS (46.62.146.40), then re-enable the proxy config in config/deploy.yml with ssl: true and the real host. Also re-enable config.assume_ssl and config.force_ssl in production.rb if not already set.
close_reason: Domain cnxmafia.org registered on Cloudflare, DNS A record pointed to VPS, deploy.yml and production.rb updated

title: Fix Kamal secrets parser incompatibility for ADMIN_EMAIL/ADMIN_PASSWORD
description: The .kamal/secrets file uses ${ADMIN_EMAIL:-} and ${ADMIN_PASSWORD:-} syntax inside $(…) blocks. Kamal's parser does NOT support ${VAR:-fallback} bash syntax, causing empty/garbage values to reach the container. This breaks db:seed and import:scrape. Fix: simplify to $(printenv ADMIN_EMAIL) and $(printenv ADMIN_PASSWORD) pattern.

title: Exclude test gems from production Docker image
description: Dockerfile sets BUNDLE_WITHOUT=development but does not exclude the test group. This installs capybara, selenium-webdriver, mutant-rspec, simplecov, and many other test-only gems in the production image unnecessarily. Fix: change to BUNDLE_WITHOUT=development:test.

title: Custom Devise views with Tailwind styling
description: Generate custom Devise views (rails generate devise:views) and restyle them to match the site's dark Tailwind design. Covers: sessions/new (login), registrations/new (register), registrations/edit (account settings), passwords/new (forgot password), passwords/edit (reset password). Each view should use consistent form styling with the rest of the app. No need for confirmable/lockable/omniauth views since those modules are not enabled.
close_reason: Already implemented and merged to master

title: Allow claimed player name editing with uniqueness validation
description: The Player.name field serves as the player's nickname. When a user claims a player, they should be able to edit the name via their profile page (vanilla-mafia-51). Add validates :name, uniqueness: true to Player model (if not already present). The profile edit form should include the name field. This ties into vanilla-mafia-51 (ProfilesController) which already handles the edit view.

title: Refactor hardcoded season navigation to be dynamic
description: The layout currently hardcodes seasons 1..5 in both the sidebar and mobile nav. This should be dynamic — either query Season/Game for distinct seasons or use a helper. When season 6 starts, the nav should update automatically.

title: Add English locale keys for custom Devise views
description: The custom Devise views (sessions/new, registrations/new, registrations/edit, passwords/new, passwords/edit) use i18n keys like devise.sessions.new.title, devise.registrations.new.title, etc. Russian translations exist in config/locales/devise.ru.yml but the English equivalents are missing from config/locales/devise.en.yml. Add the matching English keys so views render correctly when locale is :en.

title: Re-enable assume_ssl and force_ssl after HTTPS is configured
description: Once a real domain with HTTPS is pointed at the server, uncomment config.assume_ssl, config.force_ssl, and config.ssl_options in config/environments/production.rb. Also re-enable the SSL proxy in config/deploy.yml (see commit d48338d). These were disabled because Secure cookies break CSRF over plain HTTP (PR #88).
close_reason: SSL enabled in deploy.yml, assume_ssl and force_ssl re-enabled in production.rb

title: Configure I18n with Russian locale
description: Set default locale to :ru, create config/locales/ru.yml with all model attributes and page strings

title: Switch deploy runner to ARM to eliminate QEMU emulation
description: The deploy workflow runs on ubuntu-latest (x86_64) but builds arm64 images for the Hetzner ARM VPS. This forces QEMU emulation, which is 5-10x slower than native and requires -j 1 workarounds for bootsnap. Switch to ubuntu-24.04-arm runner in .github/workflows/deploy.yml to build natively. Expected to save ~10 minutes per deploy.

title: Add Docker layer caching to deploy workflow
description: Every deploy rebuilds all Docker layers from scratch (apt-get, bundle install, bootsnap precompile, assets:precompile). Add Docker build cache using docker/build-push-action with GitHub Actions cache (cache-from/cache-to type=gha). This way unchanged layers are reused between deploys, saving ~3-5 minutes when only application code changes.

title: Feature toggle system
description: Implement a lightweight feature toggle mechanism for the app. Research alternatives to Flipper (which has concerning pricing). Consider building a simple custom solution — e.g. a FeatureToggle model backed by a DB table with key/value pairs and a simple API like Feature.enabled?(:require_approval). Should replace the current Rails.application.config-based approach used in PlayerClaim.require_approval? and be extensible for future flags.

title: Add Docker layer caching to deploy workflow
description: Configure Kamal builder to use GitHub Actions cache (type=gha) and add required setup steps to the deploy workflow. This reuses unchanged Docker layers between deploys.
close_reason: Added GHA cache config to Kamal builder and setup steps to deploy workflow

title: User locale switching
description: Allow users to switch between Russian and English locales. Parent feature issue. Sub-tasks: locale column on users, set I18n.locale per request, language switcher UI in layout.

title: Add locale column to users table
description: Add migration for locale string column on users (default: 'ru', null: false). Validate inclusion in %w[ru en]. Update user factory.

title: Set I18n.locale per request from user preference or cookie
description: Add a SetLocale concern in ApplicationController that sets I18n.locale on each request. Authenticated users: read from user.locale. Guests: read from a 'locale' cookie (default to :ru).

title: Language switcher UI in layout
description: Add a language toggle (RU/EN) to the application layout — both sidebar and mobile nav. Clicking switches locale: for authenticated users updates user.locale + cookie, for guests sets cookie. Redirect back to current page after switching.

title: Judge role & standalone protocol form
description: Replace admin boolean with role enum (user/judge/admin), move protocol form to /judge/protocols, add nav link for judges and admins
close_reason: Implemented judge role enum, standalone protocol form at /judge/protocols, nav link for judges/admins

title: News model + migration
description: Create News model with title, published_at, status enum (draft/published), game_id FK, author_id FK. GH #185.

title: Create all database migrations
description: Create migrations for players, roles, games, ratings, awards, player_awards with proper constraints and indexes

title: Editor role for User model
description: Add editor role to User model's role system. GH #186.

title: Tag model and polymorphic Tagging join model
description: Create Tag model (name, slug) and Tagging join model (polymorphic). GH #187.

title: News authorization policies
description: Authorization policies for news: admins/editors CRUD, users/guests view published only. GH #188.

title: News admin controller
description: CRUD actions for news in admin namespace. Draft/publish workflow. GH #189.

title: News admin form view
description: Form with title, rich text body, game selector, tags, publish toggle. GH #190.

title: News admin list view
description: List of news articles in admin. Filter by status, paginated. GH #191.

title: Public news index page
description: Paginated list of published news. Filterable by tag. GH #192.

title: News show page
description: Individual article view with full rich text body, linked game, tags. GH #193.

title: Homepage news feed section
description: Latest N published news on homepage. GH #194.

title: Game detail page - linked news section
description: Show news articles linked to a game on the game detail page. GH #195.
